title: Review of DOC-401: System Architecture Document v1.0 — REV-631
text:
# Review Record REV-631

Document: DOC-401 (System Architecture Document v1.0). Reviewer: Dr. Rhea Voss (Chief Systems Engineer). Date: 2023-03-28. Overall disposition: Approved with comments.

Status: Closed.

## Comments

**C01** (Dr. Rhea Voss): Section 3.2 — Autonomy level selection. The document records our decision to target SAE Level 3 autonomy (conditional automation) rather than Level 4. This was not purely a technical decision. During the WIKI-003 kick-off meeting (Feb 2023) we had a specific customer requirement (Ref: customer RFT-K200-002) that prohibited fully autonomous navigation in mixed-traffic maritime zones without a human supervisory override. The rationale for Level 3 must be stated explicitly in DOC-401 §3.2 so that future engineers do not attempt to re-litigate this as a cost-saving measure. The regulatory constraint is real and binds the architecture. ECN-301 captures the formal configuration baseline. — *Action required*

**C02** (Dr. Rhea Voss): Section 4.1 — Redundancy philosophy. I am approving the single-string GNC architecture (no FCU redundancy) on the condition that the mission profile is restricted to BVLOS operations over open water only, with a safe-return-to-launch fail-safe. The rationale: adding a second FCU (P-1091 duplicate) would add ~300g and exceed the 8kg MTOW constraint. This trade was discussed at WIKI-003 and is documented in the risk register under R-GNC-004. Anyone proposing to extend K-200 operations to urban or littoral zones must revisit this trade. The current architecture is NOT safe for operations near infrastructure. — *Action required*

**C03** (Dr. Rhea Voss): Section 5.3 — Datalink range. The 15km datalink range requirement drives the choice of 900MHz band and the 1W transmit power in P-1125. This is the tightest constraint on the comms architecture. If the range requirement ever increases, the antenna gain, frequency, or power budget must all be re-analysed. Do not change P-1125 without also updating DOC-471 (Comms ICD) and the link budget in DOC-401 §5.3. — *Noted*

**C04** (Dr. Rhea Voss): Section 7 — Weight budget. The current mass allocation shows 340g margin at MTOW 8kg. This is uncomfortably tight. I have flagged this as a programme risk (R-SYS-001 in the risk register). The margin must not be eroded by scope creep in payload or battery. — *Accepted*

comments.author: E01
comments.text: Section 3.2 — SAE Level 3 autonomy was chosen due to customer RFT-K200-002 prohibiting fully autonomous navigation in mixed-traffic maritime zones. Rationale from WIKI-003 kick-off meeting must be preserved in DOC-401 §3.2. ECN-301 captures the formal baseline.

comments.author: E01
comments.text: Section 4.1 — Single-string GNC architecture approved only for BVLOS open-water operations. Adding second FCU (P-1091) exceeds 8kg MTOW. Trade documented in WIKI-003 and risk register R-GNC-004. Urban/littoral extension requires architecture review.

comments.author: E01
comments.text: Section 5.3 — 15km range drives 900MHz / 1W choice in P-1125. Any range increase requires re-analysis of antenna, frequency, and power budget. DOC-471 must be updated together with DOC-401 §5.3.

comments.author: E01
comments.text: Section 7 — 340g mass margin is tight. Flagged as programme risk R-SYS-001.
